question: Will GLW's market close price on 2026-06-28 be higher than its market close price on 2026-06-21? Stock splits and reverse splits will be accounted for in resolving this question. Forecasts on questions about companies that have been delisted (through mergers or bankruptcy) will resolve to their final close price. Resolution criteria: Resolves to the market close price at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/GLW for the resolution date. If the resolution date coincides with a day the market is closed (weekend, holiday, etc.) the previous market close price is used. Background: Corning Incorporated operates in optical communications, display, specialty materials, automotive, and life sciences businesses in the United States, Canada, Mexico, Japan, Taiwan, China, South Korea, Germany, and internationally. The company provides optical fibers and cables; and hardware and equipment products, such as cable assemblies, fiber optic hardware and connectors, optical components and couplers, closures, network interface devices, and other accessories for the telecommunications industry, businesses, governments, and individuals. It also offers glass substrates for flat panel displays, including liquid crystal displays and organic light-emitting diodes that are used in televisions, notebook computers, desktop monitors, tablets, and handheld devices. In addition, it manufactures products that offer material formulations for glass, glass ceramics, crystals, precision metrology instruments, and software, as well as glass wafers and substrates, tinted sunglasses, and radiation shielding products for markets, such as mobile consumer electronics, semiconductor equipment optics and consumables, aerospace and defense optics, radiation shielding products, sunglasses, and telecommunications components. Further, the company provides ceramic substrates and filter products for emissions control in mobile, gasoline, and diesel applications, as well as technical glass and optic products and solutions for the interior and exterior of vehicles. Additionally, it offers laboratory products, including plastic vessels, liquid handling plastics, specialty surfaces, cell culture media, and serum, as well as general labware, and glassware and equipment under the Corning, Falcon, PYREX, and Axygen brands. It also offers polysilicon products and pharmaceutical glass tubing and vials. The company was formerly known as Corning Glass Works and changed its name to Corning Incorporated in April 1989. Corning Incorporated was founded in 1851 and is headquartered in Corning, New York.

evidences.title: Corning (GLW) Q1 2026 Earnings Analysis: Massive Beat but Stock ...
evidences.content: The Q1 2026 Earnings Beat: Core Sales reached $4.35 billion (an 18% YoY increase), while Core EPS surged 30% to $0.70, beating the $0.69 consensus. Optical Communications Boom: Segment sales skyrocketed 36% YoY to $1.85 billion, fueled by Gen AI demand and two newly announced long-term agreements with hyperscale customers. Solar Segment Drag: While the new Solar segment saw sales jump 80% to $370 million, an extended maintenance shutdown at Corning's wafer facility will create a $30 million incremental expense headwind in Q2. The Springboard Plan: Corning details how it has grown core sales by 33% and EPS by 79% since Q4 2023, and previews its upcoming May 6 Investor Event in NYC, where management plans to extend this growth plan through 2030. evidences.title: Corning Incorporated (GLW) Fair Value Analysis (2026)
evidences.content: ## Executive Summary Based on an analysis as of November 4, 2025, Corning Incorporated (GLW) appears significantly overvalued. With its stock price at $90.29, it is trading at the very top of its 52-week range of $37.31 to $91.93. Key valuation metrics supporting this view include a high trailing twelve-month (TTM) P/E ratio of 57.91, an elevated EV/EBITDA multiple of 23.14, and a low free cash flow (FCF) yield of just 1.59%. These figures are substantially higher than both historical averages and peer benchmarks in the Electronic Components industry, suggesting the current stock price has outpaced the company's fundamental earnings and cash flow generation. The investor takeaway is negative, as the stock's valuation appears stretched, implying a high risk of price correction. [...] Corning's valuation multiples are considerably elevated compared to industry norms. Its TTM P/E ratio is 57.91, while the forward P/E (based on next year's earnings estimates) is a still-high 30.83. The US Electronic industry average P/E is around 24.3x, making GLW appear expensive on a relative basis. evidences.content: ### Earnings vs. Estimates Corning posted core earnings per share (EPS) of $0.70 for the quarter ended March 31, 2026, compared to the analyst consensus estimate of $0.6968. This represents a 30% increase year-over-year, marking a clear beat on the bottom line. On the revenue front, core sales reached $4.345 billion, coming in slightly ahead of the estimated $4.306 billion. This was an 18% increase from the $3.679 billion reported in the first quarter of 2025. While both headline numbers cleared analyst projections, the narrow margin of the beat appears to have disappointed investors who had priced in more substantial upside given the company’s strong momentum in AI-related products. ### Key Press-Release Highlights [...] AI-Driven Growth: The company highlighted robust demand for Gen AI products as a primary growth driver. Optical Communications sales surged 36% year-over-year, while Solar sales jumped 80%. New Hyperscaler Agreements: Corning announced it finalized two additional long-term agreements with hyperscale customers, similar in size and duration to the previously announced multiyear, up-to-$6 billion deal with Meta. Dividend policy typically covers the current dividend amount per share, payment frequency (commonly quarterly), and the yield calculated relative to share price. Investors seeking income from corning stock monitor dividend yield and dividend sustainability indicators such as payout ratio and free cash flow coverage. Ex-dividend dates and record dates are announced in company communications. ### Share repurchases [...] ### Trading milestones and splits Throughout its history, Corning has passed price milestones reflecting new product ramps, cyclical recoveries, and macro events. Stock splits, if enacted historically, would be noted in company filings and investor-relations timelines. Investors should consult Corning’s investor-relations archive and SEC filings for a tabulated record of dividends, splits, and major corporate events. ## Business segments and how they affect the stock Corning’s revenue and profitability are influenced by the relative performance of its business segments. If negative, this analysis would suggest an expected loss by executing the strategy. | [...] on the $90 strike and the $110 strike, and buy on the $85 strike and the $115 strike Long Stock Only: what would have happened if you simply bought the underlying stock and no options. | Historical Open Range The start day of the strategy timeframe, relative to the earnings date. If the earnings results are released before market open (BMO), the day of earnings trading would be the same calendar date. If results are released after the close (AMC), the day of earnigns would be the next trading day. If earnings is released Tuesday AMC, the Day of Earnings would be Wednesday, and 1 Day Before would be Tuesday. | Historical Close Range The end day of the strategy timeframe, relative to the earnings date. evidences.content: expanded-beam ferrule technical An expanded-beam ferrule is the front tip of a fiber-optic connector that widens and focuses the light beam before it crosses a connection, making physical contact less critical and the joint far more tolerant of dust, dirt, and misalignment. For investors, that means equipment using this technology tends to be more reliable and lower-maintenance in harsh or mobile environments (military, industrial, telecom), which can reduce downtime and support premium pricing or steady recurring revenue for makers and users. fiber-to-the-home technical [...] optical fiber communication technical Optical fiber communication is a way of sending information as pulses of light through thin glass or plastic fibers, like using a string of tiny, fast-moving flashlights to carry data. It matters to investors because it underpins high-speed internet and data services, influences where telecom and cloud companies spend capital, and can affect growth, competitive advantage, and long-term infrastructure costs in technology and communications markets. See more from StockTitan in Google Search and AI answers. Adds StockTitan as a preferred source · opens Google Add on Googl...

evidences.title: GLW Stock 2026: Corning's AI Photonics Boom Explained
evidences.content: GLW Stock 2026: Corning’s AI Data Center Photonics Boom is one of the clearest examples of how the AI trade is expanding beyond semiconductors. Corning’s opportunity sits in the optical layer of AI infrastructure: fiber, cable, connectivity, photonics, and manufacturing scale. The bull case is supported by strong optical demand, hyperscaler agreements, and a strategic push into Photonics MAP. But the best research must stay balanced. GLW is not a pure AI stock, valuation matters, and the company must execute on capacity expansion while proving that AI data center demand can translate into durable earnings growth. Investors should track Optical Communications performance, margin conversion, customer concentration, free cash flow, and AI capex trends before forming a view. [...] `GLW` At SimianX AI, stock research is most useful when it combines fundamentals, market context, technical momentum, valuation, news catalysts, and risk debate. This GLW stock 2026 research article applies that multi-signal mindset to Corning’s AI photonics opportunity. evidences.title: Corning Announces Strong First-Quarter 2026 Financial Results (1)
evidences.content: News Summary: First-quarter core sales grew 18% to $4.35 billion, and core EPS grew 30% to $0.70 versus Q1-2025. Robust demand for Gen AI products and the ramp of new solar products drove Q1 growth. Optical Communications sales grew 36%, and Solar sales were up 80% year over year. Two additional hyperscale customers entered into large, long-term agreements with Corning. Deals are similar in size and duration to the recently announced multiyear, up-to-$6 billion agreement with Meta. Agreements highlight customers’ commitment to develop, innovate, and manufacture the critical technologies that power next-generation AI data centers in the United States. [...] Corning plans to upgrade and extend its Springboard plan through 2030 and introduce a new Market-Access Platform at the company’s May 6 NYC investor event. Optical Communications’ new Photonics Market-Access Platform will serve Gen AI OEM customers. [...] market needs, while also helping its customers capture new opportunities in dynamic industries. Today, Corning’s markets include optical communications, mobile consumer electronics, display, automotive, solar, semiconductors, and life sciences.
